business.type_label: academic admissions consulting firm (medical school + college)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to trust a person/firm with a high-stakes expert service (getting into med school/college): the page leans on founder credentials, advisor bios, 20+ years, 90%+ success rate, and 14 testimonials. Sells defined expertise → trust, first rung of the ladder.

goal.one_action: book a complimentary consultation (or submit contact form) to enter the 1:1 consulting funnel

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page consultancy: home routes to three pathways (1:1 consulting, free resource libraries, downloadable guides); two deep service landing pages (med / college) each carry full trust argument + pricing table + FAQ; contact page offers form + Calendly.

mapped.observed_as: Looking for a trusted advisor to help you get into medical school?
How we can help you get into medical school
What differentiates us from other med school admissions consultants
Some of the med schools to which our students have been accepted / acceptance messages
Ready to learn which comprehensive plan is right for you? / What to do next

unmapped.observed: Meet some of our medical school / college admissions advisors
unmapped.why_no_fit: A team/advisor profile grid; closest canonical is a team block but sectionList has no dedicated team id

confidence.why: fetch_quality is partial — contact/booking form fields and Calendly/Google-Form embeds were not rendered/readable, so exact conversion-surface fields are inferred, not observed. Section/engine classification is otherwise well-supported by the captured page structure.

gap_findings: Team/advisor profile grid ('Meet our advisors') has no canonical section id; mapped to founderNote as closest but it is really a multi-person credential block distinct from a single founder note.

Pricing shown ($7,999-$24,999) but no checkout — high-ticket consultancy where pricing is displayed for qualification, then conversion is a booked consultation; 'packages' capability covers the display but the display-price-without-cart pattern for services is a recurring shape.
